mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production, as measured by the INDPRO index, indicates expanding manufacturing output and overall economic strength, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as corporate earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: The Rank IC of INDPRO changes versus 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any rolling 2-year window, or the signal produces negative average returns in expanding periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and declining consumer income, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the signal duration has fewer than 20 occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate is below 0.5% and year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 2%, real interest rates are negative, providing a stimulative monetary environment that boosts equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces non-positive average forward returns (p > 0.10) or the frequency of the condition is less than 5% of samples.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle economic expansions, robust payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) indicates increasing labor demand that pushes up wages and eventually margins but initially lifts equity valuations over the next quarter, as expectations of future earnings improve.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative average 60-day returns during periods classified as late-cycle expansion, or the correlation between PAYEMS changes and forward returns is not positive in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) greater than 1% signals a sudden drop in consumer spending, indicating economic weakness that depresses S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: When RSAFS MoM decline >1%, the average 5-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05), or the signal is observed fewer than 10 times.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) are above 1.1 million annualized units and the federal funds rate is below 2%, it indicates a healthy housing market supported by accommodative monetary policy, which is positive for the overall economy and S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields non-positive average 60-day returns (p > 0.10) or the condition occurs less than 10% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles (consecutive FEDFUNDS rate hikes), the expectation of higher discount rates and reduced liquidity exerts downward pressure on equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified tightening cycles, the average 20-day forward return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the definition of tightening cycle fails to identify any periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) greater than 0.5% signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity, which triggers negative market sentiment and lower S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: When INDPRO MoM decline exceeds 0.5%, the average 5-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative (p > 0.05), or the signal occurs fewer than 15 times in the sample.
